subject: ILM
what_happened: George Lucas formally signs Industrial Light & Magic into existence.
people: George Lucas; John Dykstra

subject: Van Nuys warehouse
what_happened: Dykstra locates and leases the Valjean Avenue warehouse in Van Nuys, establishing the future model-shop and shooting-stage site.
people: John Dykstra

subject: overall effects unit
what_happened: Dykstra is brought in to supervise special photographic effects; the scale of the planned effects program is already understood as unusually large.
people: John Dykstra; George Lucas; Gary Kurtz
place: pre-Van Nuys planning and Van Nuys ramp-up

subject: initial core crew
what_happened: Early ILM recruiting brings in the core technical and model crew, including Grant McCune, Bill and Jamie Shourt, Richard Edlund, Al Miller, Richard Alexander, and Don Trumbull.
people: Dykstra; McCune; Shourts; Edlund; Miller; Alexander; Don Trumbull; Bob Shepherd; Jerry Greenwood

subject: departments and stages
what_happened: Over roughly six weeks, the empty warehouse is physically converted into a model shop, machine shop, wood shop, optical and rotoscope areas, production offices, and rear shooting stages.
people: ILM core crew

subject: motion-control system
what_happened: Dykstra states the Dykstraflex design was completed in July 1975, after which construction of the system began.
people: John Dykstra; Al Miller and team

subject: optical animation art
what_happened: Robbie Blalack and Adam Beckett join by July; by early August Joe Johnston is setting up the art department.
people: Robbie Blalack; Adam Beckett; Joe Johnston; Ben Burtt

subject: bidding boards and storyboards
what_happened: Paul Huston starts in August 1975, initially helping Joe Johnston produce bidding/story boards while the model shop is only just getting going.
people: Paul Huston; Joe Johnston; Jamie Shourt

subject: Escape Pod
what_happened: A known production blueprint is printed Johnston 9/25/75, making the Escape Pod one of the earliest precisely date-anchored miniature-design elements in the ANH corpus.
people: Joe Johnston

subject: X-Wing filming design
what_happened: ILM moves from concept X-wings toward buildable filming miniatures: fuselage bucks, armatures, wing mechanisms, and revised shell geometry are being worked out in Van Nuys.
people: David Beasley; Grant McCune; David Grell; Joe Johnston; Dave Jones and others

subject: first ILM Y-Wing
what_happened: The first ILM Y-wing, later called Red Jammer or Red Y-Wing, exists by December 1975 and is sent incomplete to England so the full-size prop builders can blueprint and mock up the 1:1 set piece.
people: early Y-wing crew; likely Joe Johnston / model-shop staff

subject: Blue 1 X-wing
what_happened: The first completed ILM X-wing, Blue 1, is detailed and painted just before Christmas and shipped to production designer John Barry at Elstree on December 26, 1975 as cockpit/set reference.
people: Lorne Peterson; Jon Erland; Steve Gawley; Joe Johnston; John Barry

subject: Fox financing and schedule disruption
what_happened: 20th Century Fox hesitation in late 1975 causes pre-production disruption; by Lucasfilm's later account this contributes to a hurried final stretch of preparation before principal photography.
people: George Lucas; Fox; production leadership

subject: Blue 1 to Red 2
what_happened: After returning from England in the new year, Blue 1 is completed with cockpit, pilot, and electrical finishing and redressed into the Red 2 hero X-wing.
people: Grant McCune; Joe Johnston; Steve Gawley and team

subject: whole Van Nuys pipeline
what_happened: Dykstra later says ILM brought the Van Nuys facility from an empty warehouse to a full in-house effects system in eight months, implying a major operational ramp from summer 1975 into roughly early 1976.
people: Dykstra-led ILM crew

subject: TIE Fighter
what_happened: A dated wedge marked 3/6 documents a maroon two-tone TIE test miniature under motion-blur / compositing evaluation, proving active TIE camera tests by this date.
people: original ILM test crew

subject: animation and rotoscope
what_happened: Dave Brown later states he was hired into ILM's Rotoscope & Animation department in March 1976, giving a useful internal anchor for the shop's staffing and late pre-shoot / early shoot phase.
people: Dave Brown; Adam Beckett; Mike Ross; Pete Kuran

subject: live-action production
what_happened: Principal photography on The Star Wars begins in Tunisia on March 22, 1976; by then ILM is already operating in Van Nuys on miniatures, motion control, and effects planning.
people: George Lucas; Gilbert Taylor; cast and live-action crew
place: Tunisia / Elstree, related to Van Nuys support

subject: Y-Wing program
what_happened: Heritage's Y-wing Number One account places the start of real Y-wing build work around spring 1976, with Paul Huston building the master pattern / hero buck from which the ANH Y-wing family was cast.
people: Paul Huston and model-shop crew

subject: Death Star cannon
what_happened: Paul Huston says the Death Star cannon, built with Stuart Ziff, was the key model in the first shot ILM produced. The exact date is still open, but it belongs to the early functioning phase of Van Nuys miniature photography.
people: Paul Huston; Stuart Ziff

subject: model shop as a team
what_happened: Huston describes a period after the first months of chaos when the model shop became highly efficient and new models seemed to flow out, specifically naming the Sandcrawler as a build almost everyone touched.
people: Paul Huston; broad model-shop crew

subject: opening-shot Blockade Runner
what_happened: A unique 16-inch Blockade Runner is built to create the illusion of recession in the opening shot because of the limited length of the Dykstraflex track. The exact build date still needs pinning, but its purpose is tightly defined.
people: Grant McCune collection provenance; Richard Edlund cited

subject: ANH fighter programs
what_happened: By the main ANH effects period, ILM is building not just hero miniatures but pyro families and duplicate castings for fighters, with increasingly specialized construction logic for motion-control, lighting, and destruction work.
people: model shop; mold shop; stage crew

subject: day-to-day ILM environment
what_happened: Dave Brown's 5757 footage documents the original Van Nuys facility in active use across 1976-1977, showing workspaces, parking lot culture, screening room, and the lack of air conditioning outside the optical department.
people: Dave Brown; Adam Beckett; Richard Edlund; Dennis Muren; Ken Ralston and others

subject: final effects completion
what_happened: Brown's recollection places him first in animation/rotoscope, then on camera/stage work, then in optical, where the printers eventually ran 24/7; he says he worked until about one week before the film's initial release.
people: Dave Brown; Robbie Blalack; Richard Edlund; Dennis Muren; Ken Ralston

subject: Star Wars release
what_happened: Star Wars premieres, closing the initial ANH production window while some ILM staff memories indicate effects work was still ending immediately beforehand.
people: Lucasfilm; ILM

subject: public technical account
what_happened: The July 1977 issue of American Cinematographer publishes John Dykstra's near-contemporary account of the special photographic effects program, preserving unusually valuable timing and staffing data.
people: John Dykstra
